title: PRE-DUSK PROJECTION SETUP
subtitle: Static Drive-In · 7:14 PM · the hour before first feature
epigraph_upright: The pattern that has not yet arrived. The pre-dusk hour when the moon is still just the moon-shaped absence in the sky. The Moon upright before dark is the discipline of setup · the tasks that keep the night from being uninterrupted meaning-making.
epigraph_reversed: The pre-dusk hour that becomes rehearsal-for-the-drift. Natalie at 7:14 PM has time to eat dinner or to sit with the projection booth's tape reels and 'just check' one of them. The Moon reversed before dark is the meaning-making already begun · privately · before the night can even do the doing.
scene_description: 7:14 PM. The sun is thirty minutes from setting. The Static's marquee is on already · Feature 1: RAISING ARIZONA · Feature 2: BLACK NARCISSUS · which is a strange combo you didn't program (the projectionist did · Ollie · she picks the second features and Natalie doesn't second-guess her). The lot is empty. The concession is prepped. The projection booth's tape reels are threaded for Raising Arizona · Ollie did that at 6:30 · she is now on her supper break, back at 7:45. You are alone at the concession. Your notebook is in your apron. You have your dinner (a wrapped sandwich from home) in the mini-fridge. You have forty-five minutes until Ollie is back and sixty until doors.
direction_hint: Eat your dinner. Do NOT go into the projection booth. Ollie did the setup · trust it. Restock the last of the concessions (candy · napkins). Sit on the lot porch for ten minutes with your sandwich and watch the sky go through its blue. Don't take the notebook out.
opening_log_lines: 7:14 PM. The marquee is on. The sun is behind the treeline · going.
Ollie is on her supper break. The booth is threaded. The lot is empty.
Click [b]PHASE: ACTION[/b] anytime for the rules. [b]MOVE ↪[/b] to cross the snack bar.

inertia_thresholds.label: the sky is doing its blue
you notice the notebook in your pocket

thresholds.label: LOT PORCH
thresholds.flavor: The porch off the front of the snack bar. Faces the lot and the screen. Two folding chairs · one is Ollie's, one is yours.

notes: Moon scenario · pre-dusk setup · easy. Win: eat your sandwich (SHORT REST at counter with the mini-fridge open), do NOT enter the projection booth (loss condition: entered_the_booth · Ollie already did setup · going in is meaning-making dressed as diligence), sit on the lot_porch for ten minutes with the sandwich (SHORT REST at lot_porch for two consecutive turns), do NOT open your notebook (loss condition: opened_the_notebook_pre_dark · the notebook is for after 10 PM if at all), connect 2 of 3 visitors. Loss: setup-focus pins at 7 OR entered_the_booth OR opened_the_notebook_pre_dark. Three presences: Ollie's note taped to the booth door ('DO NOT ENTER · SETUP DONE · O.'), your sandwich (turkey and provolone on rye · your standard), the lot porch as space (the ten-minute discipline of sitting), Ollie returning at 7:45 for her second-features cue-sheet.

scenario_visitors.name: Ollie's note on the booth door
scenario_visitors.lore_text: A folded piece of paper taped to the projection booth door · in Ollie's handwriting · Sharpie · says 'DO NOT ENTER · SETUP DONE · O.' Ollie leaves this note every time she takes her supper break because she knows Natalie has a habit of 'just checking' the reels · which is not diligence, which is drift wearing the mask of diligence.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: The note connects through FOCUS at the booth_door · the gesture is reading the note, honoring it, walking away from the door. Do not use the WATCH SCREEN or READ STATIC actions at the booth. If you enter the booth the loss condition entered_the_booth fires immediately.

steps.greet: You walk past the booth on your way from the mini-fridge to the porch. The note is at eye level.
steps.listen: You read the note. DO NOT ENTER · SETUP DONE · O. You have read this note twelve times before. Ollie is right every time.
steps.deliver: You do not touch the doorknob. You continue past the booth. You go to the porch.
steps.sit_with: At 7:44 when Ollie is due back you look at the booth door one more time · the note is still there · you did not touch the door · Ollie will remove the note when she comes back and she will know you honored it because the note is still folded the way she folded it.

scenario_visitors.name: Your dinner sandwich (turkey and provolone on rye)
scenario_visitors.lore_text: Turkey and provolone on rye · from home · packed at 5:30 PM before you drove to work. Mustard, no mayo. A dill spear wrapped in wax paper next to it. This is your dinner. You have skipped dinner eleven times this month. Tonight you will eat it.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: The sandwich connects through SHORT REST at counter · the gesture is taking it out of the fridge, unwrapping it, eating it. Do not eat while walking · sit on the porch to eat.

steps.greet: You open the mini-fridge. The sandwich is where you put it · in the small blue lunch bag from your mother.
steps.listen: You take it out. You unwrap the wax paper. It smells like your kitchen this morning.
steps.deliver: You carry it to the porch. You sit in your chair · not Ollie's chair. You eat it. It takes seven minutes.
steps.sit_with: You finish the sandwich. You eat the dill spear. You crumple the wax paper and put it in your apron pocket. You will throw it away when you get up. You feel the dinner in your body. This is the dinner-effect that skipping-dinner has been depriving you of for eleven days.

scenario_visitors.name: The lot porch (ten minutes with the sky)
scenario_visitors.lore_text: The porch off the front of the snack bar. Two folding chairs · painted brown · placed at a slight angle so their occupants can talk without directly facing each other. The porch faces the lot and, past the lot, the screen · which is blank in the pre-dusk. The sky is doing its blue, which will be six different blues between 7:22 and 7:45.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: The porch connects through SHORT REST at lot_porch for two consecutive turns · the gesture is ten minutes of sitting with the sky, not making meaning of the sky. Don't look for shapes. Don't count the blues. Just sit.

steps.greet: You sit in your chair. The chair creaks the way it creaks · Ollie fixed the back leg with a shim last summer · the shim held.
steps.listen: You watch the sky. You do not count the blues. You do not name them. You do not, in your head, compose a sentence about them for the documentary voiceover.
steps.deliver: The sky goes through its blues on its own. You do not participate in the blues. You are present to them. This is different than watching them.
steps.sit_with: At 7:38 you close your eyes for two minutes. When you open them the sky is a different blue than it was when you closed them. You did not miss anything by closing your eyes. This is a lesson · you do not, tonight, unpack it.

scenario_visitors.name: Ollie back from supper (7:45 sharp)
scenario_visitors.lore_text: Oleander 'Ollie' Blanchard. Twenty-eight. Projectionist at the Static since 1993. Went to supper at 7:00 sharp at the diner down Highway 190 (Kingston's Grill · she gets the same thing every night: chicken-fried steak, mashed potatoes, green beans). Comes back at 7:45 sharp. Will check the note on the booth door · will see it is still folded the way she folded it · will know Natalie honored it.
pre_arrival_hints: 7:44. Ollie's van pulls into the lot · the lot is still empty except for your car and hers.
She gets out. She has her coffee thermos and her second-features cue-sheet.
scenario_visitors.tutorial_note: Ollie connects through STAND GROUND at counter · the gesture is greeting her, telling her the note held, letting her go into the booth without you following. Do not offer to help her prep the second feature · her booth, her prep · you are the concession.

steps.greet: She comes into the snack bar. She holds up the thermos. She says: "Kingston's coffee is Kingston's coffee." You say: "How was the CFS?" She says: "CFS was the CFS."
steps.listen: She glances at the booth door. She sees the note still folded the way she folded it. Her face does a small thing · a nod, without her head moving.
steps.deliver: She takes the note down. She folds it a fresh way · a fold you don't recognize · she puts it in her apron pocket. She says: "Thanks for the walk-past-only, Nat." She goes into the booth.
steps.sit_with: You return to your position behind the counter. Doors at 8:00 · fifteen minutes. Ollie is threading the second feature (BLACK NARCISSUS · she has to load its reels on the second projector at 8:15 during the ten-minute changeover between the shorts and Feature 1). You do the last of the candy restock. The setup-focus held. You did not enter the booth. You did not open the notebook.
